title: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
description: A post-training alignment workflow that collects human preference signals, fits a reward model, and optimizes the language-model policy toward preferred behavior.
openingSummary: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback, usually shortened to RLHF, is a post-training workflow that steers model behavior using human preference signals after a base or instruction-tuned checkpoint already exists.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: RLHF is a post-training alignment method. Teams collect human rankings or scores over model outputs, fit a reward model from that feedback, and then run reinforcement learning so the policy earns higher reward on behavior people prefer.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: Instruction-tuned models can follow demonstrations, but product teams often need behavior shaped by comparative judgments: which answer is more helpful, honest, or safe. RLHF exists to turn those preference judgments into a training signal that nudges the model toward aligned behavior.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: A pretrained or instruction-tuned model generates candidate answers. Human annotators rank or score those outputs. A reward model learns to predict those preferences. A reinforcement-learning optimizer, often Proximal Policy Optimization or PPO, updates the policy so preferred answers earn higher reward while staying close to a reference model.

comparedToNearbyRegimes.title: Compared To Nearby Regimes
comparedToNearbyRegimes.body: Supervised fine-tuning and instruction tuning learn from one target answer per prompt. RLHF learns from comparative human judgments and a separate reward-model plus policy-optimization loop. Direct Preference Optimization, or DPO, can learn from preference pairs without maintaining that separate reward-model and PPO machinery.

limitationsAndFailureModes.title: Limitations And Failure Modes
limitationsAndFailureModes.body: RLHF depends on costly, inconsistent, or narrow human data. A reward model can mismatch what users actually want, and policy optimization can become unstable or overfit to the reward signal.

trainingFlow.title: RLHF training flow
trainingFlow.alt: A three-step RLHF flow: base model, human preference data and reward signal, then an aligned model after policy optimization.
trainingFlow.caption: RLHF turns human preference judgments into a reward signal and then optimizes the policy toward preferred behavior.
